rationale: Response correctly identified that Section 12 ERA 1996 concerns pay statements, not contract variation, and provided substantive employment law guidance. However, it gave a generic textbook answer without integrating any elements from the company_context block — no reference to the persona's industry (electrical components distribution), UK-only geography, or the fact that employment is not among the company's captured recurring matters (which focus on supplier/customer/warranty disputes). A well-briefed agent would have noted that this falls outside Meridian's typical commercial wheelhouse and aligned advice with the conservative risk appetite and stakeholder dynamics.
specific_gaps: No mention of industry (electrical components B2B distribution)
No acknowledgment that employment matters are not in the company's captured recurring matters
No integration of the conservative risk appetite or stakeholder dynamics (MD approval thresholds)
Response reads as generic employment law advice rather than Meridian-specific counsel
